seoTitle: New Buildings vs New Italy — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between New Buildings and New Italy. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: New Buildings vs New Italy: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Buildings and New Italy.
heading: New Buildings vs New Italy — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Buildings, New South Wales and New Italy, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Italy has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to New Buildings (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Buildings or New Italy safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Buildings has a flood risk score of 38/100, while New Italy scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Buildings scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. New Italy scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Buildings or New Italy?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Italy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
